title: Step 5: Use a catch-all slug route

content.text: Replace dedicated routes with one catch-all route. Resolve the URL from slug segments, then switch on page._type to render the correct view.

body.code:
app/
├ layout.tsx
╰ [[...slug]]/page.tsx

entries/
├ landing/
│ ├ LandingPage.tsx
│ ╰ LandingPage.schema.tsx
├ blog/
│ ├ BlogPage.tsx
│ ├ BlogPostPage.tsx
│ ├ BlogPage.schema.tsx
│ ╰ PostPage.schema.tsx
├ settings/
│ ├ SiteLayout.tsx
│ ╰ SiteLayout.schema.tsx
╰ page/
  ╰ SitePage.schema.tsx

blocks/
├ text/
│ ├ TextBlock.tsx
│ ╰ TextBlock.schema.tsx
├ image/
│ ├ ImageBlock.tsx
│ ╰ ImageBlock.schema.tsx
╰ gallery/
  ├ GalleryBlock.tsx
  ╰ GalleryBlock.schema.tsx

cms.tsx

content.text: Define a recursive SitePage entry type so editors can freely nest regular site pages.
Use a catch-all route that rebuilds the URL from slug segments and resolves the matching entry.
This route replaces app/page.tsx, app/blog/page.tsx, and app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx by branching on page._type.
